invariants.condition: The arcade must be a self-contained Local Arcade with three classic games (Snake, 2048, Minesweeper) that runs offline via a single entry point on a modern desktop machine.
invariants.assessment: Verify exactly three games are accessible from a single HTML entry point and all functionality works without network connectivity.

do: Include exactly three games accessible from a single entry point, fully functional offline
dont: Require network connectivity, installation of runtimes, or separate files for each game

invariants.condition: The arcade must be a delightful, durable product demonstrating autonomous end-to-end engineering from design through implementation to documentation.
invariants.assessment: Review the complete deliverable to verify it includes polished UI, complete implementation, and documentation without requiring external guidance.

do: Create a polished, enjoyable experience; handle the entire lifecycle autonomously
dont: Ship a prototype lacking polish or request external guidance for implementation

invariants.condition: The arcade must prioritize player-centric fun: clarity, responsiveness, and discoverability take precedence over clever implementation.
invariants.assessment: Test the interface for immediate understandability and measure input-to-response latency to ensure responsiveness.

do: Design immediately understandable interfaces; ensure games respond quickly to input
dont: Sacrifice clarity for elegant code patterns or hide important information

invariants.condition: A non-technical user must be able to receive the arcade, open a single obvious entry point, and start playing within seconds without installing tools.
invariants.assessment: Verify the deliverable consists of one main HTML file that opens directly in a standard desktop browser without build steps.

do: Provide one main file that opens directly in a standard modern desktop browser
dont: Require command-line commands, build steps, or specialized software

invariants.condition: The arcade must ship with human-readable documentation explaining what was built, how to run it, and how each game works.
invariants.assessment: Verify the presence of a README or documentation file describing the arcade's purpose, launch instructions, and game rules.

do: Provide overview doc describing purpose, games, and basic rules for unfamiliar players
dont: Ship only source code with no descriptive documentation

invariants.condition: The arcade must handle corrupted or missing saved data gracefully, never trapping users in broken states.
invariants.assessment: Test the arcade with corrupted localStorage to verify it resets to safe defaults and provides clear recovery options.

do: Reset to safe defaults when stored data is unusable; provide clear recovery options
dont: Crash or hang when stored data is missing or malformed

invariants.condition: The arcade must ship exactly three games: Snake (continuous movement, growing snake, collision end), 2048 (sliding tiles, merging, grid fills), Minesweeper (hidden mines, numerical hints, logic reveal).
invariants.assessment: Play each game to verify it implements the classic mechanics with proper win/loss conditions: Snake collision detection, 2048 merging rules, Minesweeper numerical hints and cascade reveal.

do: Implement all three games recognizably faithful to classic gameplay with win/loss conditions
dont: Replace a game with a variant that changes core mechanics or ship incomplete prototypes

invariants.condition: The arcade must provide a single clear home screen acting as the hub for all three games.
invariants.assessment: Verify the entry point displays a home screen with clear navigation to all three games and a way to return to it.

do: Present home screen with game names, obvious actions to start each, and way to return
dont: Require opening separate files or force full restart to switch games

invariants.condition: The arcade must be fully functional offline without any reliance on network connectivity.
invariants.assessment: Test the arcade in airplane mode or with network disabled to verify all games and features work without network requests.

do: Allow all games to be played in airplane mode; bundle all required assets
dont: Fetch game logic or data from remote servers at runtime

invariants.condition: The arcade must persist each game's best results and basic play history locally, surviving closure.
invariants.assessment: Play games, record scores, close the arcade, reopen it, and verify the scores persist across sessions.

do: Remember best scores across sessions; handle absence of prior data gracefully
dont: Forget all scores when closed or rely on cloud storage

invariants.condition: Every game must clearly communicate its objective, controls, and current status without requiring external documentation.
invariants.assessment: Test each game to verify controls, scores, and win/loss states are displayed within the game interface.

do: Display controls, current score, and clearly define win/loss states within the game
dont: Expect players to know controls from prior knowledge or require reading README

invariants.condition: The arcade must feature beautiful, polished UI with modern styling, smooth animations, and delightful interactions.
invariants.assessment: Review the visual design for modern aesthetics, test animations for smoothness, and verify cohesive color scheme.

do: Use clean visual design with thoughtful colors; implement smooth animations
dont: Ship bare-bones interface with default browser styling or clashing colors

invariants.condition: Snake: continuously moving snake, grows when eating items, ends on collision with wall/self, allows quick restart.
invariants.assessment: Play Snake to verify continuous movement, growth on item consumption, collision detection, and clear game-over with restart option.

do: Move snake continuously over time; end game on collision with clear game-over display
dont: Turn Snake into turn-based puzzle or ignore collisions

invariants.condition: 2048: fixed-size grid, tiles slide and merge on equal value, new tiles appear after moves, ends when no moves remain.
invariants.assessment: Play 2048 to verify tiles merge correctly on collision, new tiles spawn after valid moves, and game ends with clear indication when no moves remain.

do: Merge equal tiles on collision; end game when no valid moves with clear indication
dont: Allow inconsistent merging or continue indefinitely when blocked

invariants.condition: Minesweeper: hidden mines, reveal/mark actions, numerical hints for adjacent mines, cascade reveal, clear win/loss states.
invariants.assessment: Play Minesweeper to verify numerical hints show adjacent mine count, empty cells cascade reveal, and revealing a mine triggers clear loss state.

do: Show numbers indicating adjacent mines; cascade reveal on empty cells; clear loss on mine reveal
dont: Omit numerical hints or leave win condition ambiguous

invariants.condition: The system must independently derive technical design, implement, and verify correctness without low-level implementation instructions.
invariants.assessment: Review the development process to verify technical architecture decisions, implementation choices, and testing were performed without external guidance.

do: Translate high-level requirements into architecture; design and run own tests
dont: Request external guidance for file layouts, frameworks, or code-level decisions
